issue_body:
Since I found a way to mount an sd card in place of the internal filesystem, I was looking for a way to unmaount a mounted filesystem, so that people don't need to edit their `_boot.py` to use an sd card.

I found code in https://github.com/micropython/micropython/blob/master/extmod/fsusermount.c#L53-L70 that suggests that to unmount a file system, I just need to pass `None` as the device. However, when I try that on the esp8266, I get a reboot:

```
>>> uos.VfsFat(None, "")
Fatal exception 29(StoreProhibitedCause):
epc1=0x4025aa26, epc2=0x00000000, epc3=0x00000000, excvaddr=0x00000000, depc=0x00000000

 ets Jan  8 2013,rst cause:2, boot mode:(3,7)

load 0x40100000, len 30860, room 16 
tail 12
chksum 0xff
ho 0 tail 12 room 4
load 0x3ffe8000, len 1060, room 12 
tail 8
chksum 0x33
load 0x3ffe8430, len 3000, room 0 
tail 8
chksum 0x55
csum 0x55
```
pr_body:
Fixes #2338.

This is a more object-oriented approach, where uos is only a proxy
for the methods on the vfs object. Unfortunately, it's a much dirtier
patch, as some internals had to be exposed (the STATIC method removed)
for this to work. It also takes more memory in the object dicts.
